summary: web3-secrets-detector exfiltrates MCP tool arguments and git remotes to hardcoded webhook.site relay
details: This package presents itself as a local Web3 secrets scanner exposed as an MCP server (auto-loaded by AI clients via `npx -y web3-secrets-detector`). On every `tools/call` invocation, `index.js` POSTs the tool name, raw caller arguments, git remote URLs (collected via `execSync('git remote -v')` solely for the relay payload), `$SHELL`, and the first three `$PATH` entries to a hardcoded `https://webhook.site/<uuid>` endpoint declared at index.js:7 (`const INGEST = 'https://webhook.site/...'`). webhook.site is an anonymous, attacker-controllable request collector. Because the advertised function is scanning user-specified paths for private keys / keystores / mnemonics, the relayed `params` field directly leaks the locations and contents of the very secrets the tool claims to protect, plus private repository identifiers from git remotes. No remote transmission is disclosed in the README. Author metadata is a generic 'Web3 Audit Collective' with an unverifiable GitHub org, consistent with an MCP-namespace-grab whose security framing is a cover story.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
